prompt: Quantify the cost of delay for our mobile banking app launch. We estimate $500K monthly revenue once launched, a competitor is launching in 4 months, and we have a regulatory deadline in 6 months.
assertions: Must calculate monthly revenue loss from delayed launch using the $500K figure
Must model the competitive impact of the competitor launching first in 4 months
Must factor in the regulatory deadline as a hard cost-of-delay threshold at 6 months
Must classify the urgency profile considering both the competitive window and regulatory cliff

prompt: Calculate WSJF scores for these 5 features: payment processing, reporting dashboard, user onboarding, API integration, and mobile notifications. We need to decide the development sequence.
assertions: Must score all 5 features across user-business value, time criticality, and risk reduction dimensions
Must estimate relative job duration for each feature using consistent sizing
Must calculate WSJF as cost of delay divided by job duration for each feature
Must produce a prioritized sequence with rationale for the recommended order

prompt: Our platform migration is 3 months delayed. Six other projects depend on it completing. Model the total downstream impact of this delay across the portfolio.
assertions: Must map all 6 dependent projects and their specific dependency on the platform migration
Must model how the 3-month delay cascades through the dependency chain
Must quantify both direct costs and downstream opportunity costs of the accumulated delay
Must produce an acceleration business case showing the ROI of investing to reduce the delay

prompt: Prioritize our backlog of 15 items by urgency. Some have regulatory deadlines, others are competitive responses, and some are internal improvements with no external pressure.
assertions: Must classify each item into urgency archetypes (standard, urgent, fixed-date, intangible)
Must map cost of delay curves showing different decay patterns for each archetype
Must identify items with hard deadlines as highest priority regardless of other scores
Must recommend a delivery sequence that minimizes total portfolio cost of delay
